original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, children, and young people with established Crohn’s disease.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports symptom, relapse, treatment-monitoring, perianal, nutrition, prior surgery, patient-view, and follow-up documentation without assigning activity or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.11 — information, assessment, and monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports age-appropriate information, multidisciplinary discussion, symptoms, nutritional status, growth, treatment context, and safety-monitoring records.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.3 — remission and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording patient views, relapse symptoms, agreed follow-up, access arrangements, and surveillance status.

title: Crohn’s disease: management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adults, children, and young people with established Crohn’s disease.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
